openmeter/entitlement/balanceworker.purpose: Kafka-driven worker that recalculates entitlement balances on lifecycle events (grant created/voided, entitlement created/deleted/reset, batched ingest). Subscribes to three topics (system, ingest, balance-worker) and publishes snapshot events downstream to the notification service.

patterns.name: Two-stage recalculation via RecalculateEvent
patterns.description: Direct lifecycle events (grant, entitlement, ingest) are converted into RecalculateEvent and published back to the balance-worker topic; a second handler consumes RecalculateEvent and calls handleEntitlementEvent. This decouples event fanout from actual calculation.

patterns.name: Filter-before-calculate discipline
patterns.description: Every recalculation path calls w.filters.IsNamespaceInScope then w.filters.IsEntitlementInScope before any DB or ClickHouse access. Skipped events return (nil, nil) — not an error.
patterns.example: inScope, err := w.filters.IsNamespaceInScope(ctx, ns); if !inScope { return nil, nil }

patterns.name: handleOption functional options for event context
patterns.description: WithSource, WithEventAt, WithSourceOperation, WithRawIngestedEvents build handleEntitlementEventOptions. Always pass WithEventAt; zero eventAt fails Validate().

patterns.name: PublishIfNoError for conditional publish
patterns.description: w.opts.EventBus.WithContext(ctx).PublishIfNoError(w.handleEntitlementEvent(...)) is the canonical pattern for handlers that might return nil event (filtered) without error.

patterns.name: Options structs with Validate() on all constructors
patterns.description: WorkerOptions, RecalculatorOptions, and EntitlementFiltersConfig all implement Validate() using errors.Join. Constructors call Validate() first and return early on error.
patterns.example: if err := opts.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to validate worker options: %w", err) }

patterns.name: RecordLastCalculation after every successful snapshot
patterns.description: After creating a snapshot event, call w.filters.RecordLastCalculation with the entitlement and calculatedAt. Failure is non-fatal (warn + continue) because it only affects deduplication.

patterns.name: AddHandler for extensibility after construction
patterns.description: w.AddHandler(grouphandler.GroupEventHandler) appends additional batched-ingest handlers post-construction. Used by billing-worker to hook into ingest events without modifying balance-worker internals.

key_file_guides.role: Entry point: WorkerOptions, Worker struct, New() constructor, router setup subscribing to 3 Kafka topics, eventHandler() registering all NoPublishingHandler event type closures.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All new event type handlers must be registered inside the grouphandler.NewNoPublishingHandler call in eventHandler(). Never add handlers outside this call — w.nonPublishingHandler is set after construction and AddHandler is the extension point.

key_file_guides.role: Core calculation: handleEntitlementEvent (filter → fetch entitlement → filter → processEntitlementEntity), processEntitlementEntity (deleted/reset/normal branching), createSnapshotEvent, createDeletedSnapshotEvent, snapshotToEvent.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Reset operations use entitlementEntity.CurrentUsagePeriod.From as calculatedAt, not time.Now(). Deleted/expired entitlements emit a delete snapshot with nil Value, not an error.

key_file_guides.role: Recalculator struct for batch recalculation jobs: ListInScopeEntitlements (paginated, includes recently deleted), ProcessEntitlements, sendEntitlementEvent. Uses LRU caches for feature and customerSubject lookups.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses defaultPageSize=20_000 and paginates explicitly because Ent IN-subqueries break on large sets. DefaultIncludeDeletedDuration=24h governs how long deleted entitlements stay in scope.

key_file_guides.role: handleBatchedIngestEvent: queries BalanceWorkerRepository for affected entitlements, checks activity period overlap with event timestamps, publishes RecalculateEvent for each affected entitlement.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Entitlement activity period check (GetEntitlementActivityPeriod) gates whether an ingest event can affect the entitlement. Skip deleted entitlements silently — their final snapshot was already published.

key_file_guides.role: EntitlementFilters wraps a []NamedFilter chain (NotificationsFilter + HighWatermarkCache) with OTel counters per scope/filter/name.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Filter chain short-circuits on first false result. RecordLastCalculation calls only filters implementing CalculationTimeRecorder (optional interface). All metric counters must be initialized via WithMetrics before use.

key_file_guides.role: BalanceWorkerRepository interface + ListAffectedEntitlementsResponse DTO. GetEntitlementActivityPeriod computes the valid event-reception window (uses min of ActiveTo/DeletedAt as upper bound).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Activity period uses ActiveFrom over CreatedAt when set. When both ActiveTo and DeletedAt are present, the earlier one wins as the upper bound.

key_file_guides.role: resolveCustomerAndSubject helper: gets customer, optionally resolves first subject key from usage attribution. Subject may be nil (no usage attribution) — callers must handle nil Subject.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If cus.UsageAttribution == nil, returns (customer, nil, nil) — not an error. Used in both Worker and Recalculator; shared via package-level function, not a method.

anti_patterns: Publishing a snapshot event directly from a lifecycle event handler instead of going through RecalculateEvent — breaks the two-stage deduplication and filter pipeline
Calling handleEntitlementEvent without WithEventAt — eventAt.IsZero() causes a hard error in Validate()
Adding DB or external service calls to filter implementations without LRU+TTL caching — IsNamespaceInScope/IsEntitlementInScope are called per-event
Registering new event type handlers outside the grouphandler.NewNoPublishingHandler block in eventHandler() — they will never be called
Using time.Now() as the snapshot calculatedAt for reset operations — reset snapshots must use CurrentUsagePeriod.From to capture initial grant state

decisions.decision: Three-topic subscription (system + ingest + balance-worker) with RecalculateEvent intermediary
decisions.rationale: Separates high-volume ingest fan-out (one event → many entitlements → many RecalculateEvents) from the actual balance calculation, allowing independent retry and deduplication via high-watermark cache
Prevents unnecessary ClickHouse queries for entitlements with no active notification rules or that were recently calculated, critical for cost at scale

decisions.decision: Recalculator as separate struct from Worker
decisions.rationale: Batch recalculation jobs (cmd/jobs) need to reuse the same calculation logic without constructing a full Kafka-connected Worker, so the core logic is extracted into Recalculator

code_examples.scenario: Registering a new lifecycle event type that triggers balance recalculation

openmeter/entitlement/driver.purpose: V1 HTTP driver for the entitlement domain: adapts entitlement.Service and meteredentitlement.Connector to Chi HTTP handlers using httptransport.HandlerWithArgs, with subject-key-based customer resolution. Also provides the shared Parser singleton and MapEntitlementValueToAPI used by both v1 and v2 drivers.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs typed triplet
patterns.description: Each handler is declared as a type alias of httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[Request, Response, Params]. Request/Response/Params are defined as named types or type aliases immediately above each handler type declaration.

patterns.name: Namespace from context only
patterns.description: All handlers call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) via namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder — namespace is never accepted as a URL/query parameter in v1.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return request, err }

patterns.name: Subject-key to customer resolution in operation closure
patterns.description: Customer resolution (resolveCustomerFromSubject) happens in the operation closure, not the decoder. The decoder only reads the subject key from URL params.

patterns.name: Parser singleton for entitlement type dispatch
patterns.description: Parser.ToAPIGeneric dispatches on EntitlementType to ToMetered/ToStatic/ToBoolean. Always use Parser.ToAPIGeneric rather than switching on EntitlementType inline in handlers.

patterns.name: GetErrorEncoder() for domain error mapping
patterns.description: All handlers pass httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(GetErrorEncoder()) in their options. GetErrorEncoder maps feature.FeatureNotFoundError, entitlement.NotFoundError, AlreadyExistsError (with conflictingEntityId), InvalidValueError, InvalidFeatureError, WrongTypeError to HTTP status codes.

patterns.name: Separate connector fields for base and metered ops
patterns.description: meteredEntitlementHandler holds both entitlementConnector (entitlement.Service) for base entitlement queries and balanceConnector (meteredentitlement.Connector) for grant/balance ops. Never cross-use them.
patterns.example: h.balanceConnector.CreateGrant(...) for grant ops; h.entitlementConnector.ListEntitlements(...) for base entitlement queries

key_file_guides.role: EntitlementHandler interface + entitlementHandler struct: CreateEntitlement, OverrideEntitlement, GetEntitlement, GetEntitlementById, DeleteEntitlement, GetEntitlementValue, GetEntitlementsOfSubject, ListEntitlements.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateEntitlement populates UsageAttribution after resolving customer in the operation closure — not in the decoder. ListEntitlements supports both page-based and limit/offset pagination via commonhttp.Union response type.

key_file_guides.role: MeteredEntitlementHandler: CreateGrant, ListEntitlementGrants, ResetEntitlementUsage, GetEntitlementBalanceHistory. resolveCustomerFromSubject bridges subject key → customer ID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: resolveCustomerFromSubject checks subj.IsDeleted() before proceeding. GetEntitlementBalanceHistory maps burndown segments to GrantBurnDownHistorySegment — both windowed and burndown history are returned in one response.

key_file_guides.role: Parser singleton with ToMetered/ToStatic/ToBoolean/ToAPIGeneric for domain→API mapping. ParseAPICreateInput for API→domain. MapAPIPeriodIntervalToRecurrence and MapRecurrenceToAPI for interval conversion.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ParseAPICreateInput prunes ActiveFrom/ActiveTo explicitly (request.ActiveFrom = nil) — lifecycle fields are not accepted via the create API. MapRecurrenceToAPI is best-effort for ISO durations that don't map to named enums.

key_file_guides.role: GetErrorEncoder() returns the domain-specific error encoder for this package. Called by all handlers in both entitlement.go and metered.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AlreadyExistsError encoder adds conflictingEntityId to the response extensions map. New domain errors must be added here — omitting them causes fallthrough to generic 500.

anti_patterns: Switching on EntitlementType inline in handler closures instead of calling Parser.ToAPIGeneric
Accepting namespace as a query/path parameter — always resolve via h.namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx)
Calling balanceConnector methods for base entitlement operations or vice versa
Adding new domain error types to handler closures instead of GetErrorEncoder()
Calling entitlement service methods with a raw subject key string instead of first resolving to customer.ID

decisions.decision: Subject-key-centric v1 driver with customer resolution inside operation closure
decisions.rationale: V1 API predates customer-ID-centric v2; subject key is the public identifier in v1 but customer.ID is required internally. Resolution in the operation closure keeps the decoder stateless.
Stateless type-dispatch logic shared between v1 and v2 drivers; singleton avoids repeated construction while keeping methods organized by entitlement sub-type.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new v1 handler that reads an entitlement by customer subject and returns it

openmeter/entitlement/hooks.purpose: Organisational container for entitlement ServiceHook implementations that enforce cross-domain constraints without creating circular imports. Each child package implements models.ServiceHook[entitlement.Entitlement] for a specific external domain concern.

patterns.name: Embed NoopServiceHook, override only needed methods
patterns.description: Hook structs embed models.NoopServiceHook[entitlement.Entitlement] so only PreDelete/PreUpdate methods that need logic are overridden. Compile-time assertion var _ models.ServiceHook[entitlement.Entitlement] = (*hook)(nil) is mandatory.

patterns.name: Context-based operation gate
patterns.description: subscription.IsSubscriptionOperation(ctx) must be checked first in any PreDelete/PreUpdate override to allow the subscription service itself to bypass the guard during its own lifecycle operations.

key_file_guides.role: Implements PreDelete/PreUpdate guard: blocks deletion/mutation of subscription-annotated entitlements unless called from within a subscription operation. Returns models.GenericForbiddenError (maps to 403).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must check subscription.IsSubscriptionOperation(ctx) before checking annotation presence. Returning any error type other than GenericForbiddenError breaks HTTP status mapping.

anti_patterns: Adding hooks that import entitlement.Service (rather than the entitlement domain types) — risks import cycles
Adding PostCreate/PostUpdate with DB side-effects without verifying no import cycle with the entitlement adapter package

decisions.decision: One sub-package per external domain concern (subscription/)
decisions.rationale: Keeps each cross-domain constraint in its own compilation unit, preventing any single hook from accumulating unrelated concerns and making it easy to wire/unwire individual hooks in app/common

openmeter/entitlement/validators.purpose: Organisational container for customer.RequestValidator implementations that enforce entitlement-domain pre-mutation constraints on customer operations. Each child package prevents illegal customer lifecycle transitions (e.g. deleting a customer with active entitlements) without creating circular imports.

patterns.name: Embed customer.NoopRequestValidator
patterns.description: Validator structs embed customer.NoopRequestValidator so only ValidateDeleteCustomer (or other relevant methods) need to be overridden. Compile-time assertion is mandatory.

patterns.name: Depend on EntitlementRepo, not entitlement.Service
patterns.description: Use entitlement.EntitlementRepo for validation queries to avoid heavy dependency on the full service and to prevent import cycles.

patterns.name: Return models.GenericConflictError for blocked operations
patterns.description: The HTTP layer maps only GenericConflictError to 409. Validation failures must use this error type.

key_file_guides.role: ValidateDeleteCustomer: lists active entitlements for the customer at clock.Now(), returns GenericConflictError if any exist.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must use clock.Now() not time.Now() for test determinism. Depends on EntitlementRepo, not entitlement.Service.

anti_patterns: Importing entitlement.Service instead of entitlement.EntitlementRepo — causes heavy dependency and risks import cycles
Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() — breaks test determinism
Returning non-GenericConflictError for 'has active entitlements' — HTTP layer maps only ConflictError to 409
Adding state-mutating logic inside validator methods — validators are read-only guards, not service operations

decisions.decision: Depend on EntitlementRepo rather than entitlement.Service
decisions.rationale: RequestValidator is called from within the customer service; depending on entitlement.Service would risk import cycles since the service layer imports customer for hooks
